text:
India

Vinesh Phogat joins protesting farmers at Shambhu border (File Photo)

New Delhi:Wrestler Vinesh Phogat on Saturday joined the protesting farmers at the Shambhu border on the 200th day of the agitation. The ace wrestler backed the farmers on their key demands and also urged the government to listen to their grievances.

"It has been 200 days since they are sitting here. It is painful to see this. All of them are citizens of this country. Farmers run the country. Nothing is possible without them, not even athletes—if they don't feed us, we won't be able to compete," she said.

Phogat continued, "Many a time we are helpless and can't do anything. We represent the country at such big levels, but we can't do anything for our family even when we see them sad."

"I urge the Government that they should listen. They had admitted their mistake last time; they should fulfill the promises that they made. The country won't progress if people sit on the streets like this," she added.

The protest which enters the 200th day today began on February 13, after authorities prevented their march to Delhi. The farmers have raised the issue of Minimum Support Price (MSP) for all crops, a demand which has not yet been met by the Centre.

Meanwhile, farmers are also demanding decisive action against Bollywood actress and Member of Parliament (MP) Kangana Ranaut, urging the BJP to address her controversial remarks, which have previously provoked strong reactions from the farming community.

Kangana's remarks on the protest drew sharp responses from the farmers and the Opposition parties. The BJP too has distanced itself from her remark.

The BJP also directed the actor-turned-politician not to give such statements in future. "Whatever Kangana Ranaut has stated regarding the farmers' agitation, the party does not subscribe to her views,” the BJP said.

“The statement made by BJP MP Kangana Ranaut in the context of the farmers' movement is not the opinion of the party. The Bharatiya Janata Party expresses its disagreement with the statement made by Kangana Ranaut,” the party said in the statement.

The controversy erupted when Kangana Ranaut posted a video on her X handle, alleging that the farmers' protest could have led to anarchy in India similar to that in Bangladesh. The actor-turned-politician claimed that external forces were conspiring to destabilize India with the aid of "insiders."

Ranaut also alleged that during the farmers' protest against the now-repealed farm laws, there were instances of "bodies hanging and rapes taking place." She said that such violence was averted only due to the foresight of India's leadership.

The farmers are also expected to reveal their strategy for the upcoming Haryana elections in the near future.

Farmers are also calling for action against MP Kangana Ranaut over her controversial remarks about the protest., India News, Times Now
